title: Establish and maintain a risk management system
trigger: Deployment of high-risk AI systems
requirement: The provider must establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system in relation to high-risk AI systems.
sourceQuote: A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems.

title: Conduct regular systematic review and updating of the risk management system
trigger: Throughout the lifecycle of high-risk AI systems
requirement: The provider must conduct a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of high-risk AI systems, requiring regular systematic review and updating.
sourceQuote: The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review and updating.

title: Ensure residual risks are acceptable
trigger: Adoption of risk management measures
requirement: The provider must ensure that the relevant residual risk associated with each hazard, as well as the overall residual risk of the high-risk AI systems is judged to be acceptable.
sourceQuote: In identifying the most appropriate risk management measures, the following shall be ensured: (a) elimination or reduction of risks identified and evaluated pursuant to paragraph 2 in as far as technically feasible through adequate design and development of the high-risk AI system;

title: Data Quality Criteria
trigger: when developing high-risk AI systems using techniques involving the training of AI models with data
requirement: Develop training, validation and testing data sets that meet quality criteria as per paragraphs 2 to 5.
sourceQuote: High-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of AI models with data shall be developed on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5 whenever such data sets are used.

title: Data Governance Practices
trigger: when developing high-risk AI systems using techniques involving the training of AI models with data
requirement: Implement data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose, covering design choices, data collection, preparation, assumptions, bias assessment, mitigation, and data gaps.
sourceQuote: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. Those practices shall concern in particular:

title: Bias Detection and Correction
trigger: when processing special categories of personal data for bias detection and correction
requirement: Ensure that bias detection and correction cannot be effectively fulfilled by other means, implement technical limitations, secure the data, restrict access to authorized persons, delete data once bias is corrected or retention period ends, and document reasons for processing.
sourceQuote: To the extent that it is strictly necessary for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to the high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article, the providers of such systems may exceptionally process special categories of personal data, subject to appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons.

title: Automatic Recording of Events
trigger: Deployment of a high-risk AI system
requirement: The system must automatically record events (logs) over its lifetime.
sourceQuote: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.

title: Logging Relevant Events
trigger: Deployment of a high-risk AI system
requirement: The logging capabilities must enable the recording of events relevant for identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk within the meaning of Article 79(1) or in a substantial modification.
sourceQuote: In order to ensure a level of traceability of the functioning of a high-risk AI system that is appropriate to the intended purpose of the system, logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for:

title: Minimum Logging Requirements
trigger: Deployment of a high-risk AI system referred to in point 1 (a) of Annex III
requirement: The logging capabilities must provide, at a minimum, the period of each use of the system (start date and time and end date and time of each use), the reference database against which input data has been checked by the system, the input data for which the search has led to a match, and the identification of the natural persons involved in the verification of the results.
sourceQuote: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1 (a), of Annex III, the logging capabilities shall provide, at a minimum:

title: Provide identity and contact details
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: The provider must include the identity and contact details of the provider in the instructions for use.
sourceQuote: the identity and the contact details of the provider and, where applicable, of its authorised representative;

title: Provide characteristics and limitations of performance
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: The provider must include information about the characteristics, capabilities, and limitations of the high-risk AI system in the instructions for use.
sourceQuote: the characteristics, capabilities and limitations of performance of the high-risk AI system, including:

title: Provide information to enable interpretation of output
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: The provider must include information in the instructions for use that enables deployers to interpret the output of the high-risk AI system and use it appropriately.
sourceQuote: where applicable, information to enable deployers to interpret the output of the high-risk AI system and use it appropriately;

title: Ensure human oversight tools are available
trigger: when designing and developing high-risk AI systems
requirement: include appropriate human-machine interface tools that enable effective human oversight during use.
sourceQuote: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.

title: Provide deployers with necessary oversight capabilities
trigger: when providing high-risk AI systems to deployers
requirement: ensure that deployers have the means and tools to properly understand, monitor, and control the system.
sourceQuote: For the purpose of implementing paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, the high-risk AI system shall be provided to the deployer in such a way that natural persons to whom human oversight is assigned are enabled, as appropriate and proportionate:

title: Require separate verification for certain systems
trigger: when deploying high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1(a) of Annex III
requirement: ensure that no action or decision is taken by the deployer based on the system's identification unless it has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with necessary competence, training, and authority.
sourceQuote: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1(a) of Annex III, the measures referred to in paragraph 3 of this Article shall be such as to ensure that, in addition, no action or decision is taken by the deployer on the basis of the identification resulting from the system unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority.

title: Declare accuracy and robustness metrics
trigger: Deployment of high-risk AI systems
requirement: Declare the levels of accuracy and relevant accuracy metrics in the accompanying instructions of use.
sourceQuote: The levels of accuracy and the relevant accuracy metrics of high-risk AI systems shall be declared in the accompanying instructions of use.

title: Ensure robustness against errors, faults or inconsistencies
trigger: Deployment of high-risk AI systems
requirement: Implement technical and organisational measures to ensure resilience against errors, faults or inconsistencies.
sourceQuote: High-risk AI systems shall be as resilient as possible regarding errors, faults or inconsistencies that may occur within the system or the environment in which the system operates.

title: Protect against unauthorized alterations of use, outputs or performance
trigger: Deployment of high-risk AI systems
requirement: Implement technical solutions to ensure cybersecurity that are appropriate to the relevant circumstances and risks.
sourceQuote: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities.

title: Assign human oversight
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority, as well as the necessary support.
sourceQuote: Deployers shall assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority, as well as the necessary support.

title: Monitor AI system operation
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system on the basis of the instructions for use and, where relevant, inform providers in accordance with Article 72.
sourceQuote: Deployers shall monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system on the basis of the instructions for use and, where relevant, inform providers in accordance with Article 72.

title: Keep logs for at least six months
trigger: when deploying a high-risk AI system
requirement: keep the logs automatically generated by that high-risk AI system to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months.
sourceQuote: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall keep the logs automatically generated by that high-risk AI system to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at le

title: Transparency Obligation for AI Systems Interacting Directly with Natural Persons
trigger: AI system intended to interact directly with natural persons
requirement: Ensure that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use.
sourceQuote: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect, taking into account the circumstances and the context of use.

title: Marking Synthetic Content
trigger: AI system generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content
requirement: Ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.
sourceQuote: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.

title: Disclosure for Deep Fakes
trigger: AI system generating or manipulating image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake
requirement: Disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
sourceQuote: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated.
